title: Software
text:
R packages for political science and public policy research, published on CRAN and GitHub.



pinochet
Data about the victims of the Pinochet regime, 1973–1990 – demographic details, type of violence, and geographic coordinates.
With Lucas Mingardi and Robert McDonnell.

Website GitHub CRAN





congressbr
Downloads, unpacks, and tidies legislative data from the Brazilian Federal Senate and Chamber of Deputies – legislators, bills, votes, and parliamentary activity.
With Robert McDonnell and Guilherme Duarte. Latin American Research Review, 2019.

GitHub Paper





prisonbrief
Returns tidy data from the World Prison Brief website – prison populations, occupancy rates, and demographic breakdowns.
With Robert McDonnell. Journal of Open Source Software, 2018.

Website GitHub Paper

title: Danilo Freire
text:
Danilo Freire

Visiting Assistant Professor · Data & Decision Sciences · Emory University

Welcome! I am a visiting assistant professor at Emory’s Department of Data and Decision Sciences, where I teach courses on experimental methods, data science computing, and AI for the social sciences.
My research looks at how developing countries solve collective action problems and reduce political violence, mostly in Latin America. I use survey and field experiments, machine learning, and administrative data, and my papers have appeared in journals including the American Journal of Political Science and the British Journal of Political Science. I am also an EGAP Fellow and an Honorary Senior Fellow at the University of Lincoln.
You can reach me at danilo.freire@emory.edu or find my code on GitHub.



  
    
    Research
    Articles, working papers, and replication files.
  
  
    
    Teaching
    Courses at Emory and elsewhere, with syllabi.
  
  
    
    CV
    Full academic history, grants, and references.

title: Research
text: I study political violence, public policy, organised crime, and collective action, mostly in Latin American settings. On the methods side, I work with survey and field experiments, impact evaluation, meta-analysis, and computational social science tools for administrative data.

title: Teaching
text: Courses in experimental methods, data science, and AI for the social sciences, with previous teaching at Lincoln, Brown, and King’s College London.

title: Teaching
text:
Current courses




DATASCI 385 – Experimental Methods Emory

2024 to present. How to design and analyse experiments in the social sciences. We cover randomisation, power analysis, and causal inference, with worked examples in R.






DATASCI 350 – Data Science Computing Emory

2024 to present. Computational tools for data science. Topics include version control, the command line, cloud computing, and reproducible workflows.






DATASCI 185 – Introduction to AI Applications Emory

2025 to present. An introduction to AI for undergraduates. We look at how large language models work, how to write effective prompts, and how to think about ethics, bias, policy, and governance.






DATASCI 151 – Introduction to Statistical Computing II Emory

2024 to present. Python programming for data analysis. The course walks through data wrangling, visualisation, and basic statistical modelling.

title: Teaching
text:
Workshops




EGAP Learning Days 15: Field Experiments in the Social Sciences EGAP

Medellín, August 2024. Taught with Rosario Aguilar, Jake Bowers, Luis Maldonado, and Julia Seither.






EGAP Learning Days 13: Field Experiments in the Social Sciences EGAP

Mexico City, August 2023. Taught with Rosario Aguilar, Jake Bowers, Luis Maldonado, and Maarten Voors.

title: Teaching
text:
Previous courses




Policing Crime and Deviance Lincoln

2021 to 2024. Theories of policing, criminal behaviour, and deviance, with attention to accountability, legitimacy, and comparative systems. Co-taught with Anthony Ellis and Elena Gaschino.






Human Rights Lincoln

2021 to 2024. Foundations, institutions, and contemporary debates in human rights, drawing on political theory, international law, and empirical case studies. Co-taught with Nick Cowen and Aris Trantidis.






POLS 1824W – Political Violence Brown

Fall 2019. Causes and consequences of political violence. Topics include civil wars, terrorism, state repression, and organised crime.






POLS 1600 – Political Research Methods Brown

Winter 2019. Research design and quantitative methods for political science. The course covers measurement, inference, and data analysis in R.






Political Economy and Institutions OMMA / UFM

2017. Graduate seminar on institutional economics, property rights, and the political foundations of economic development.






Principles of Economics (I & II) King’s College London

2017. Teaching assistant for microeconomics and macroeconomics, covering markets, firm behaviour, and monetary and fiscal policy. With David Skarbek and Konstantinos Matakos.
